title: Assessment Items - Bomb Threat Response (Code Black)

items.question: What is the first required action when a bomb threat report is received?
choices: Evacuate the whole facility immediately without collecting any information.
Rapidly verify the source of the report and collect the initial available information related to the threat.
Ignore the report unless a suspicious object is visible.
Send maintenance staff to inspect the area first.
items.rationale: The procedure starts with immediate verification of the report source and initial information gathering.

items.question: Who must be notified immediately after the initial report is received?
choices: Only the reception desk.
Relevant specialized authorities through approved emergency numbers and facility leadership.
Visitors in the parking area.
The cafeteria supervisor.
items.rationale: The procedure requires immediate notification of specialized authorities and communication with facility leadership.

items.question: How is Code Black activated inside the facility?
choices: By sending handwritten notices to departments.
Through facility leadership coordination and announcement via the internal overhead speaker system.
Only by text message from the maintenance office.
By waiting for public media confirmation.
items.rationale: The procedure refers to coordination with leadership for announcing Code Black through the overhead speaker system.

items.question: What must security teams do regarding specialist instructions during a bomb threat incident?
choices: Follow only internal guesses if specialists are delayed.
Strictly follow all directions and technical guidance issued by specialists responsible for this type of incident.
Create their own disposal plan independently.
Ignore specialist guidance if the area looks safe.
items.rationale: The procedure requires precise adherence to specialist guidance to reduce possible risk.

items.question: What is the role of the CCTV monitoring team after the threat is reported?
choices: Shut down all cameras to protect confidentiality.
Monitor all possible locations related to the reported threat according to the initial available information.
Use cameras only after evacuation is completed.
Record only the building entrance.
items.rationale: The procedure requires monitoring possible sites linked to the threat using CCTV based on initial information.

items.question: What communication restriction applies to security personnel moving toward a suspected site?
choices: They may use any communication device as long as they speak quietly.
They should not use wired or wireless communication devices in order to avoid triggering potential risks.
They should use all radios continuously to maintain awareness.
Only mobile phones are prohibited, but radios are encouraged.
items.rationale: The policy explicitly prohibits use of wired and wireless communication devices near the suspected site.

items.question: What should security personnel do if they encounter a suspicious object?
choices: Move it carefully to a safer location.
Open it to identify the source of the threat.
Avoid moving, examining, opening, or approaching it until specialists arrive.
Take photographs from close range for faster analysis.
items.rationale: The procedure warns against moving, examining, opening, or approaching suspicious objects.

items.question: What is the correct site-control action for a suspected area?
choices: Leave the area open so people can exit faster through it.
Establish a perimeter around the suspected area, prevent entry, and maintain isolation until specialists begin their tasks.
Allow only staff with ID cards to pass through.
Move the perimeter only after media arrive.
items.rationale: The suspected area must be isolated and protected from access until specialists take over.

items.question: When should evacuation be executed during a bomb threat incident?
choices: Only after police confirm the device is real.
When directed by incident command and according to the severity and location of the threat.
Never, because evacuation increases panic.
Only after the final report is written.
items.rationale: Evacuation is executed based on incident command direction and the threat situation.

items.question: What is expected from security teams while specialized authorities are handling the event?
choices: Coordinate and cooperate with specialized authorities according to the approved protocol.
Withdraw completely and stop all support.
Continue independent action without coordination.
Prioritize media statements over response support.
items.rationale: The procedure requires ongoing coordination and cooperation with specialized authorities.

items.question: What should the operations room continue doing during the incident?
choices: Stop all updates until the incident ends.
Track security updates, operational information, and incident developments and relay them to relevant leadership and response authorities.
Share unverified rumors to prepare departments.
Focus only on visitor movement.
items.rationale: The procedure requires continuous tracking of developments and communication with leadership and response الجهات.

items.question: When may activity return to the incident area?
choices: As soon as security believes the area looks safe.
Only after the event has ended and formal approval has been issued by the relevant specialists.
Immediately after evacuation is completed.
As soon as the area is quieter.
items.rationale: Return to activity requires the event to end and formal approval from relevant specialists.

items.question: What reporting action is required after the incident?
choices: No report is required if no device was found.
Prepare and submit a joint incident report with facility specialists and specialized security authorities to facility leadership.
Write only a short verbal summary.
Document the event only in CCTV logs.
items.rationale: The procedure requires a joint incident report after the event.

items.question: Which organization must receive incident information after the event?
choices: National Center for Emergency and Health Crisis Management
Parking Department
Cafeteria Services
Outpatient Registration
items.rationale: The final step is to provide the National Center with the required incident information.
